title: SAMRIDH
text: 147173e17ea741687247 SAMRIDH SAMRIDH SCHEME Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) MeitY Startup Hub (MSH), Digital India Corporation MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) CURRENT_SCHEME_INFORMATION_AVAILABLE APPLICATION_STATUS_REQUIRES_VERIFICATION Support existing and upcoming accelerators to select and accelerate promising product-based and IT-based startups, helping them scale through customer connections, investor connections, product enhancement and international-market access. Direct scheme partners are existing or upcoming accelerators operating in India. The scheme document requires relevant incubation or accelerator experience, startup-support track record, suitable infrastructure, mentor and investor networks, and capability to run structured cohorts. Startups participate as downstream beneficiaries through accelerators selected under cohort-specific calls. Eligible accelerators as direct scheme partners and selected product-based or IT-based startups as downstream beneficiaries. Potential technology and software-product startups requiring acceleration from product or prototype development towards commercialization and growth. Selected startups may receive customized acceleration services, customer and investor connections, mentoring, legal and product support, market-access assistance and matching funding support under an approved SAMRIDH cohort. 4000000 INR Government startup investment may be up to INR 40 lakh, with the scheme document indicating an average of approximately INR 30 lakh per startup per cohort. Matching investment and final support depend on the selected accelerator, startup growth stage and cohort terms. MeitY Startup Hub or its implementing arrangement issues a cohort-specific Expression of Interest or call for accelerators. Eligible accelerators apply and undergo expert evaluation. Selected accelerators then operate cohorts and select eligible startups through their governed screening process. No currently open SAMRIDH application window has been verified. https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh https://msh.meity.gov.in/assets/Scheme-Report.pdf https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh https://msh.meity.gov.in/assets/Scheme-Report.pdf https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh 2026-07-12 0.77 0.95 NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW Permanent scheme identity retained. Funding up to INR 40 lakh is scheme-level startup support delivered through selected accelerators and must not be interpreted as an automatically available grant. No currently open cohort or application URL is recorded. Cohorts and application windows must remain separate call instances.

title: TIDE 2.0
text: 6af79cf6c8a213dddce8 TIDE 2.0 TIDE 2.0 SCHEME Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) Innovation and IPR Division, MeitY, in association with 51 incubation centres MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) HISTORICAL_INFORMATION_ONLY NO_CURRENT_APPLICATION_ROUTE_CONFIRMED Promote technology entrepreneurship through financial and technical support to incubators supporting ICT and emerging-technology startups addressing areas of societal relevance. The approved implementation model operated through designated incubation centres at institutes of higher learning and research organisations. Downstream support was intended for technology startups and entrepreneurs working with emerging technologies in approved thematic areas. No current application eligibility or open application window has been verified. Designated incubation centres, technology entrepreneurs and ICT or emerging-technology startups supported through those centres. Idea to proof of concept through Entrepreneur-in-Residence support; prototype development through grants; and product development or market outreach through investment support. Financial and technical incubation support, mentoring, ecosystem linkages and stage-based startup assistance through approved TIDE 2.0 incubation centres. 4000000 INR The administrative approval provided support up to INR 4 lakh for Entrepreneur-in-Residence idea-to-proof-of-concept activity, up to INR 7 lakh for prototype-development grants and up to INR 40 lakh for product-development and market-outreach investment. These were historical stage-specific limits, not one universal grant. During the approved implementation period, eligible entrepreneurs and startups accessed support through participating TIDE 2.0 incubation centres. The permanent scheme record does not provide a currently open central application route. https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/tide https://msh.meity.gov.in/assets/Administrative%20Approval_TIDE%202.0.pdf https://msh.meity.gov.in/assets/Administrative%20Approval_TIDE%202.0.pdf https://msh.meity.gov.in/assets/Administrative%20Approval_TIDE%202.0.pdf https://msh.meity.gov.in/assets/Administrative%20Approval_TIDE%202.0.pdf https://msh.meity.gov.in/assets/Administrative%20Approval_TIDE%202.0.pdf 2024-01-31 2026-07-12 0.85 0.99 NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW Permanent historical scheme identity retained. The approved implementation period ended on 31 January 2024. Stage-specific funding limits are recorded as historical scheme provisions. No official extension or current application route has been verified. The unrelated 29 March 2023 page date and MSH logo URL remain excluded.

STARTUPS 6 1 4 9 + INCUBATORS 5 1 8 + MENTORS 4 8 6 + ACCELERATORS 3 6 + COE 4 3 + LABS 3 2 9 + VENUE SPACES 7 6 + CO-WORKING SPACES 1 3 2 + EQUIPMENTS 8 7 1 + BOOKING REQUESTS 1 7 3 + ABOUT MSH India is home to one of the most vibrant startup ecosystems with close to 30,000+ tech startups, making it the 3rd largest startup ecosystem in the world. Hence, innovation and entrepreneurship is the emerging focus area that is being aggressively promoted to give fillip to the Indian economy. Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India is leading and facilitating a gamut of Innovation and IPR-related activities across the country towards the expansion of this ecosystem. text: meity_d09656085af5414ea7e5 CALL_INSTANCE_CANDIDATE 0.78 MeityStartupHub https://msh.meity.gov.in/about/organisationprofile https://msh.meity.gov.in/about/organisationprofile https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh rendered_dom 147173e17ea741687247 CALL_STATUS_REQUIRES_DATE_VALIDATION 200 text/html 1 0 43 0 0 True CALL_COHORT_CHALLENGE_SIGNAL True NON_CATALOGUE 0.8 The provided page is an about us or organizational profile page and does not contain information specific to a permanent scheme or programme. 2026-07-13T06:54:26+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN MeitY Startup Hub India is among top 3 startup ecosystems in the world. In order to boost the Indian economy, innovation and entrepreneurship in the emerging focus area that is being aggressively promoted to give fillip to the Indian economy. A variety of innovation and IPR-related activities are being led and facilitated nationwide by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, in an effort to grow this ecosystem. In order to facilitate MeitY’s vision of promoting technology innovation, start-ups and creation of Intellectual Properties, a nodal entity called ‘MeitY Start-up Hub’ (MSH) has been setup under its aegis. MeitY Startup Hub (MSH), is a unique, high-impact, digitally-driven scheme which seeks to strengthen the startup ecosystem by integrating the resources, upskilling the entrepreneurs, leveraging the experts, facilitating the knowledge transactions, and promoting the investments in deep-tech startups to support inclusive & sustainable techno-socio-economic development across India and international expansion programs for startups among other activities. To support and leverage the startup ecosystem we host Startups, Incubators, Centres of Entrepreneurship (CoEs), Accelerators, Mentors and Investor. MSH is actively engaged in conducting different Programs, Challenges and Hackathons, both supported and hosted in conjunction with Government bodies, Ministries, Corporates etc. in addition to capacity building workshops across a plethora of topics of interest to our beneficiaries, hosted on our one stop digital platform on Youtube called "MSH Startup School." text: meity_d21e57fa37ee3754ba4c CALL_INSTANCE_CANDIDATE 0.78 MeityStartupHub Welcome to the G20 Digital Innovation Alliance Summit! https://msh.meity.gov.in/g20dia/g20diaoverview https://msh.meity.gov.in/g20dia/g20diaoverview https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh rendered_dom 147173e17ea741687247 CALL_STATUS_REQUIRES_DATE_VALIDATION 200 text/html 1 0 48 0 0 True CALL_COHORT_CHALLENGE_SIGNAL True NON_CATALOGUE 0.8 The provided page does not contain information related to a specific scheme or programme but rather an overview of the G20 Digital Innovation Alliance Summit. 2026-07-13T06:56:06+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN G20 DIA > G20 DIA OVERVIEW G20- Digital Innovation Alliance Welcome to the G20 Digital Innovation Alliance Summit! India's presidency of the G20 Summit is a source of immense pride. As part of this presidency, the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, and the MeitY Start-up Hub are organizing the G20 Digital Innovation Alliance Summit in Bengaluru at the Bangalore Palace, from August 17th to 19th, 2023. With the focal theme, 'Driving Digital Innovation for World Economies', the Summit anticipates substantial participation from delegates representing the G20 countries and 9 guest countries, including policymakers, business leaders, technocrats, young innovators, and investors. Event Theme: Driving Digital Innovation for World Economies This theme highlights the importance of harnessing the power of Digital Innovation to propel economic growth, foster sustainable development and address global challenges. By driving digital innovation, the summit aims to facilitate collaboration, knowledge sharing, and policy discussions among G20 member countries and guest countries. The theme recognizes the critical need to leverage emerging technologies, foster digital inclusion, and build resilient digital infrastructure to unlock the full potential of economies and drive prosperity in the digital age. VENUE: Bengaluru Palace G20 Digital India Innovation Summit will be held at the historic Bangalore Palace, a Tudor-style palace located in the heart of the city. The sprawling Palace was built in 1887 as a summer residence of the Royal Family of Mysore, India. The palace has hosted many international events and concerts. The Palace interiors are open to visitors. Quick Links Broucher Exhibitor Manual Floor Plan Exhibitor Directory Indian Startup Ecosystem India is now the hub of 'Start-up' ecosystem in the world, ranking third with 90,000 'Startups' and 107 unicorn companies worth $30 billion. The Indian startup ecosystem is built on several key pillars, including government support, access to capital, a growing talent pool, and a supportive culture for entrepreneurship. Another important factor contributing to the growth of startups in India is the role played by incubators and accelerators. These organizations provide startups with access to resources and support, including mentoring, funding, and networking opportunities. One of the most important factors contributing to the growth of startups in India is access to capital. While funding was once a major challenge for startups in India, the situation has improved significantly in recent years. There are now a growing number of venture capital firms and angel investors in India, and the country has seen a surge in startup funding over the past few years. Bringing together start-ups, Central & State government agencies, corporates, research and development institutions, and academia from G20 member countries and 9 guest countries, the conference will serve as a platform for global digital advancements. Key Focus Sectors Winner of G20-DIA Global Pitching Edtech Sector Startup Rank Country Startup Name Founder Name Description Winner Republic of Korea Mathpresso Jose Jeong Mathpresso is an edtech startup running an educational platform called “Qanda”, an AI-based learning app that allows students of all levels to receive instant answers and customized learning sources where students can upload pictures of math problems when they need help and mobile tutors send them solutions. It aims to create a platform for personalized education by collecting big data through Qanda and providing content that is tailored for each student. 1st Runner Up Turkey Dusyeri Ayşe Şule Bilgiç With this application, users can scan the QR codes on the Dusyeri branded books, games, and all other products and view their contents easily. This app has thousands of content scientifically created especially for children by experts as a result of 3 years of study in a single application. 2nd Runner Up India Filo edtech Pvt. Ltd Imbesat Ahmad, Rohit Kumar & Shadman Anwer Filo is the world’s only live instant tutoring app where students are connected with expert tutors in less than 60 seconds for 1-1, interactive video sessions. More than 1.5 million students across 15+ countries are using Filo to make their everyday learning better. text: meity_b83d32f40aad208fa7e2 PROGRAMME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.9 MeityStartupHub MEITY STARTUP HUB PARTNERS WITH STATE BANK OF INDIA https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/SBI https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/SBI https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh rendered_dom STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 1 0 43 0 0 True OFFICIAL_PROGRAMME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:52:30+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > SBI MEITY STARTUP HUB PARTNERS WITH STATE BANK OF INDIA ENABLING STARTUP GROWTH THROUGH STRATEGIC FINANCIAL ACCESS As part of its mission to build a robust and inclusive startup ecosystem, MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) has partnered with the State Bank of India (SBI) — India’s largest public sector bank — to empower startups with the right mix of financial access, strategic mentorship, and ecosystem enablement. BUILDING AWARENESS: BEYOND BANKING SBI joins hands with MSH not just as a banking institution, but as a committed growth partner. Through this collaboration, startups will benefit from: Access to financial literacy programs and mentorship Exposure to market insights and capital-readiness support Guidance on financial planning, compliance, and risk management This awareness-focused approach ensures that founders make informed, confident financial decisions as they scale. ENHANCING ACCESSIBILITY: FINANCING WHAT’S NEXT With this partnership, startups can tap into SBI’s startup-centric offerings, including: Equity-linked funding opportunities Tailored credit lines and working capital support Support through SBI’s pan-India branch and digital network A STEP TOWARD INCLUSIVE STARTUP GROWTH By bridging the gap between innovation and financial infrastructure, the MSH x SBI partnership is set to power a new wave of entrepreneurship, from metropolitan hubs to Tier II/III cities.Together, we aim to create an enabling environment where startups are not only funded, but also mentored, connected, and equipped for sustainable growth. Are you a startup looking to scale with the right financial partner? Explore tailored offerings and startup-focused solutions from SBI here: SBI Startup Portal. This is your gateway to growth GET IN TOUCH Looking to explore SBI’s startup offerings or need personalized assistance? Connect with dedicated SBI Startup Branches, listed below to get the right guidance and support tailored to your startup’s needs. text: meity_d7781f7865cb61e6e99f PROGRAMME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.9 MeityStartupHub GOOGLE APPSCALE ACADEMY 2023 IMPACT https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/googleappscale https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/googleappscale https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh rendered_dom STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 1 0 44 0 0 True OFFICIAL_PROGRAMME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:53:07+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > GOOGLE APPSCALE ACADEMY 2023 IMPACT GOOGLE APPSCALE ACADEMY 2023 IMPACT Google Appscale Academy the Class of 2023, was executed with 100 startups selected from over 950 applications, represents a diverse range of sectors, including education, health, social impact, gaming, agriculture, and more. These innovative startups have harnessed technology to address critical needs, making a tangible impact on society. Appscale Academy has been instrumental in training and mentoring early to mid-stage startups, empowering them to build high-quality apps for both local and global markets. A comprehensive Google Appscale Academy 2023 VC Handbook showcasing the success stories of the program. The VC handbook serves as a consolidated repository, featuring details of all the selected startups. Indian developers continue to push boundaries by building innovative solutions for many complex challenges. Be it apps supporting diverse communities or global games emerging from beyond metro cities, we’re seeing inspiring local developers across the country underscore India’s rise as a powerful app development hub. Indian developers continue to push boundaries by building innovative solutions for many complex challenges. Be it apps supporting diverse communities or global games emerging from beyond metro cities, we’re seeing inspiring local developers across the country underscore India’s rise as a powerful app development hub. The program is outlined with a customized curriculum, including virtual instructor-led webinars, self-learning material, and mentorship sessions with leading local and global professionals from the industry. he curated, 6-month training program will identify, support and nurture promising Indian startups to supercharge their growth on Android. GOOGLE APPSCALE ACADEMY 2023 ROADSHOWS To build general awareness for the program amongst the broader ecosystem, especially in beyondmetro cities and supercharge their growth on Android, MeitY Startup Hub and Google organized 5 roadshows in 5 cities (Surat, Indore, Coimbatore, Jaipur, Gangtok) with the 5 incubation centres as implementation partners (AIC-RAISE - Coimbatore, Incub8 - Surat, Headstart, SGSITS-Shri Govindram Institute of Technology and Science - Indore, JECRC - Jaipur Engineering College and Research Centre, Atal Incubation Centre Sikkim Manipal University Technology Business Incubation Foundation (AIC-SMUTBI) - Gangtok). GOOGLE APPSCALE ACADEMY 2023 TOP 100 REVEAL EVENT AT INDIA HABITAT CENTRE Hon'ble Joint Secretary, MeitY, Shri Akash Tripathi unveiled the class of 2023 of Appscale Academy 2023. He added "India, currently holding the prestigious Presidency of G20 nations, has grown from being the tenth largest economy ten years ago to the fifth largest today. India has been the fastest growing major economy for the third year in a row—assured for further growth, driven strongly by, among other factors, a vigorous and diverse technology ecosystem. Google and MeitY's partnership through MSH have added a lot of value to the Indian startup ecosystem. My Best wishes to the Class of 2023" The chosen early to mid-stage startups will be guided by Appscale Academy to build high-quality apps for the world. The 100 startups were chosen from over 950 applications following an in-depth selection process that considered their creative ideas and innovation, product quality, product scalability, and talent diversity. 50% of the cohort representing major sectors like education, health, social, agriculture and more come from beyond metro cities including Varanasi, Ernakulam,Surat, Dehradun, Allahabad and more. Addressing the Top 100 startups selected for the program, CEO, MSH, Mr. Jeet Vijay added, "India offers rainbow of opportunities for the startups and entrepreneurs. India is showing way for the world with its dominance in digital public goods. India Stack, CoWin, Aadhar, UPI, Digilocker etc. are the biggest contributions of digital public goods that India can make to the world. More importantly this makes it possible for every society and economy to be more inclusive. It’s about tech that can be used for everyone in the world. It’s about a common man being able to use the power of technology and do something that is useful to them." text: meity_71e95141c1a17726fc50 PROGRAMME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.9 MeityStartupHub MEITY STARTUP HUB AND MATHWORKS PARTNER TO ACCELERATE DEEP TECH INNOVATION https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/mathworks https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/mathworks https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh rendered_dom STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 1 0 43 0 0 True OFFICIAL_PROGRAMME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:52:48+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > MATHWORKS MEITY STARTUP HUB AND MATHWORKS PARTNER TO ACCELERATE DEEP TECH INNOVATION MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) has signed a Statement of Intent (SoI) with MathWorks to bolster India’s deep tech startup ecosystem, providing access to state-of-the-art computational tools, advanced simulation models, and world-class training resources. As part of this partnership, MathWorks will provide complimentary access to over 100 products under its Accelerator Program, including MATLAB® and Simulink, for 12 months to all MeitY associated startups. This invaluable resource will empower startups to develop and refine their innovations with powerful computational capabilities. Beyond software access, participating startups will benefit from Technical Mentorship: Expert guidance to navigate complex technical challenges. Specialized Training: Tailored programs to maximize the utilization of MathWorks tools. Discounted Professional Access: Continued access to MathWorks products at preferential rates post-program. To further enhance industry engagement, MSH and MathWorks will conduct collaborative physical workshops at leading incubation centres across India. These workshops will empower startups, researchers, and industry professionals with the practical skills needed to address real-world challenges and accelerate innovation. Furthermore, MathWorks will offer advanced training at discounted fees, supporting faster innovation cycles and market access. Startups will also benefit from participation in MathWorks-organized events, such as MATLAB EXPO and webinars, along with opportunities to showcase their solutions on global platforms. We are thrilled to partner with MeitY Startup Hub to empower deep tech startups driving innovation in electrification, automation, and AI,” said Mr. Sunil Motwani, Country Manager, MathWorks India. “Through our tools and expertise, we aim to accelerate the development of transformative technologies that will shape the future of mobility and beyond. At MSH, we are excited to announce our commitment to empowering startups with advanced MathWorks products,” said Mr. Pannerselvam, CEO, MSH. “This partnership will support innovations that will provide startups with a significant edge in key technologies such as Artificial Intelligence, Wireless Communication, and Automation. This strategic deep tech partnership bridges the gap between technology and entrepreneurship, providing Indian startups with the essential resources to innovate, scale, and tackle global challenges. Together, MSH and MathWorks are building a thriving, future-ready startup ecosystem in India. text: meity_449512a9f8c4292c6029 PROGRAMME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.9 MeityStartupHub MEITY STARTUP HUB (MSH), MICRON AND PIEDS, CREST (BITS PILANI), LAUNCH A SEMICONDUCTOR ACCELERATOR FOR EARLY-STAGE STARTUPS https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/micron https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/micron https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh rendered_dom STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 1 0 43 0 0 True OFFICIAL_PROGRAMME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:52:54+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > MICRON MEITY STARTUP HUB (MSH), MICRON AND PIEDS, CREST (BITS PILANI), LAUNCH A SEMICONDUCTOR ACCELERATOR FOR EARLY-STAGE STARTUPS MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) an independent division of the Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India in partnership with Micron and PIEDS, the Technology Business Incubator and Center for Research Excellence in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST) of BITS Pilani announced today the launch of CREST accelerator program for startups in Semiconductor domain. The accelerator program will curate a cohort of startups and provide each with funding of up to Rs. 10 Lacs, with an opportunity to raise further investment at the end of the program. Mentors and advisors from Micron, MSH, and BITS Pilani shall guide the startups. The accelerator is being launched on the heels of the government’s big push to make India a semiconductor hub and an ambitious India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) by MeitY. The startups shall be able to use tools, labs, and facilities at Micron facilities and at the Centre for Research and Entrepreneurship in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST), BITS Pilani, Hyderabad campus, and the network of MeitY supported labs and facilities across India. Shri Rajnish Kumar, CEO, MeitY Startup Hub, said, “We congratulate the PIDES team for their initiative to support semiconductor startups with funding, mentorship, and access to Micron’s facilities for product development. Together with Micron, we are committed to nurturing the next generation of pioneers in semiconductor technology” Sachin Arya, Head, PIEDS said, “The startups will play an integral role in India’s ambitious Semiconductor Mission, and further strengthen India’s indigenous design and development capabilities. We are excited to support promising startups in Semiconductors and utilize BITS Pilani’s capabilities in Semiconductor research and innovation for overall ecosystem development.” Prof. Sanket Goel, Dean (Research & Innovation) and Convenor, CREST, said, “The Semiconductor Accelerator, will bridge the critical gap between innovative ideas and market reality for early-stage startups in chip design. By leveraging resources, mentorship, and industry connections, this dynamic program will empower students and recent graduate entrepreneurs to thrive. By providing access to seed funding, state-of-the-art labs, and a collaborative learning environment, the Accelerator will foster the next generation of innovators, shaping the future of this transformative technology.” Interested startups can apply to the CREST accelerator program by logging on to the MSH’s website https://msh.meity.gov.in/ PIEDS PIED Society is a leading platform that helps entrepreneurs get access to resources that can help them come together and innovate the future of India. It is a Technology Business Incubator at BITS Pilani which was set up in 2004 with support from Department of Science and Technology, Government of India, and have incubated many campus and alumni driven startups. With a mission to support every innovative idea that can scale to be successful, PIED Society works with startups from all BITS campuses, its alumni, and beyond. MICRON Micron is a world leader in innovative memory solutions that transform how the world uses information. For over 45 years, Micron has been instrumental to the world’s most significant technology advancements, delivering optimal memory and storage systems for a broad range of applications. CREST With a vision to make BITS Pilani as the leading academic institution for Semiconductor Education, Research and Innovation in India, Center for Research Excellence in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST) was launched in 2023. Since then, CREST is on the forefront of diversified activities, including professional development courses, hands-on training programs, minor-and-major degree programs, and organizing lectures. CREST houses labs in the entire value-chain of semiconductors, and supports faculty members, PhD scholars and interns to implement many innovative projects, and collaborated with various international and national academic institutes and industry. More details: https://crest.bits-pilani.ac.in/. text: meity_ac41fd36e3f6654e08f5 PROGRAMME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.9 MeityStartupHub About the program https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/xr-startup-program https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshcorporate/xr-startup-program https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh rendered_dom STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 1 0 44 0 0 True OFFICIAL_PROGRAMME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:53:31+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN Shortlisted Startups XR Startup Program A program to discover, nurture, and accelerate XR technology startups across India. About the program XR Startup Program is a collaboration between Meta and MeitY Startup Hub (MSH). The program aims to accelerate India’s contribution towards building the foundations of the metaverse and nurturing the development of Extended Reality (XR) technologies in India. The metaverse is the next generation of the internet. It will be a set of digital spaces, including 3D experiences that are all interconnected so people can easily move between them and will let people do things they could not do in the physical world. While the defining quality of the metaverse will be a feeling of presence, the defining technologies of the metaverse will be Extended Reality (XR), including Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR) and Mixed Reality (MR). Click here to read more Accelerator About the Accelerator The Accelerator will nurture and foster 40 early-stage startups working with XR technologies through a well-designed 6-month program. Each startup will get access to a number of benefits, including a grant of ₹ 20,00,000, mentorship by industry experts and researchers, access to a network of potential strategic partners and investors, technology and infrastructure support, and opportunities to participate and present at relevant industry events. North zone - FITT South zone - FITT East zone - FITT West zone - FITT Grand Challenge About the Grand Challenge The Grand Challenge is aimed at supporting early-stage innovators to upscale from the R&D phase to developing workable prototypes and Minimum Viable Products (MVPs). It will include 4 sector-specific challenges to advance the XR technology ecosystem in India. A total of 80 shortlisted innovators will receive a grant of ₹ 50,000 and an opportunity to attend the bootcamp. Of the 80 shortlisted innovators, 16 innovators will be supported with grants worth ₹ 20,00,000 to help them develop MVP(s)/ prototypes. Benefits include mentorship by industry experts and researchers, access to a network of potential strategic partners and investors, technology and infrastructure support, and opportunities to participate and present at industry events. text: meity_5abfa15d098bd8a59403 PROGRAMME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.9 MeityStartupHub https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshinternational/brussels https://msh.meity.gov.in/program/mshinternational/brussels https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh rendered_dom STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 1 0 48 0 0 True OFFICIAL_PROGRAMME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:53:44+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN As part of its mission to accelerate international market access and foster long-term global partnerships, MeitY Startup Hub led a high-impact delegation of Indian deep-tech startups to Brussels from May 26–29, 2025, under the MeitY Global Startup Bridge Program. This edition aimed to connect Indian startups with the European innovation ecosystem through cross-border collaboration, policy engagement, and knowledge exchange.The program focused on enabling market access, forging corporate and government partnerships, unlocking investment opportunities, facilitating technology exchange, and deepening ecosystem engagement. The Brussels edition reaffirmed MeitY Startup Hub’s vision of enabling Indian startups to scale globally through strategic partnerships, international exposure, and ecosystem-level collaboration. With the MeitY Global Startup Bridge initiative, MeitY Startup Hub positions itself as a strategic facilitator, bridging Indian deep-tech startups with the global market. The initiative addresses core startup needs such as international market access, investor and corporate connections, regulatory guidance, R&D collaboration, and ecosystem visibility. But beyond access and funding, the MeitY Startup Hub also opens doors to the right people, platforms, and partnerships, ensuring startups are not just supported, but strategically connected to scale globally. If you're a startup looking to grow beyond borders, not just through research or capital, but by plugging into the right global networks, this is your gateway. The signing of MoU between MeitY Startup Hub and hub.brussels at the Indian Embassy, by Dr. Paneerselvam Madanagopal, CEO, MeitY Startup Hub, and Mr. Bart Vandeputte, Trade and Invest Commissioner, hub.brussels, in the presence of Ambassador Shri Saurabh Kumar, underscoring a shared commitment to foster India–Belgium innovation partnerships. A key highlight was the signing of an MoU between Indian startup IG Drones and Belgian firm VoxelSensors, aimed at advancing bilateral collaboration in drone and sensor technologies. - Click on the tabs to know more: Why Brussels? text: meity_28ac6fb921386a6968c7 SCHEME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.96 MeityStartupHub https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/genesis https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/genesis seed STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 0 0 46 0 0 True OFFICIAL_SCHEME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:51:44+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN SCHEMES > GENESIS Gen-Next Support for Innovative Startups (GENESIS) With an aim to boost the startup ecosystem in Tier-II & Tier-III cities in the country, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has approved implementation of the ‘Gen-Next Support for Innovative Startups (GENESIS)’ Scheme with a budgetary outlay of Rs. 490 Crore for a duration of 5 years. The scheme envisages further scaling up and sustaining the startup ecosystem especially to discover, grow and nurture technology startups. The scheme aims to support approx. 1600 tech start-ups over the course of next 5 years List of selected Implementing Agencies Major objectives of the Scheme: Consolidation of startup related schemes and assets of MeitY for greater efficiency, asset utilization and enhanced support for startups. Provide Tier-II/ III cities focused funding to critically support Pilot/ Investment, Early-stage and deep-tech startups. Strengthening Incubators, building stronger startup ecosystem via capacity building initiatives in Tier II/ III cities. Major components of GENESIS: Entrepreneur in Residence (EiR) Support to students and entrepreneurs to validate their startup ideas so as to create momentum in Tier II/ III cities for entrepreneurship. Pilot Funding Support to startups who have validated their idea and need to pilot their product. Investment Support to startups that are raising funds from the market and this will bring domestic rupee capital into the Tier II/ III cities across India. Deep-Tech Funding Support to a select number of startups that are developing high impact solutions. text: 147173e17ea741687247 147173e17ea741687247 SCHEME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.96 MeityStartupHub https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/samridh seed STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 0 0 46 0 0 True https://api.meity.gov.in/user/profileimage?type=undefined; https://api.meity.gov.in/sitevisitors; https://api.meity.gov.in/flagshipevents?page=0; https://api.meity.gov.in/allschemes OFFICIAL_SCHEME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:51:20+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN SCHEMES > SAMRIDH RESULT OF SAMRIDH COHORT 2 Startup Accelerator of MeitY for Product Innovation, Development, and Growth (SAMRIDH) The Government of India has launched a comprehensive initiative to foster innovation and support start-ups through its Startup Accelerator of MeitY for Product Innovation, Development, and Growth (SAMRIDH) program, addressing the need for an accelerator program that drives social impact and scales solutions for India's challenges. The program aims to support 300 tech Start-Ups in 3-year duration with cohort size of 5-10 Start-Ups. SAMRIDH Program is conceptualized and being implemented to provide support to selected accelerators for extending acceleration services to start-ups along with one-to-one matching funding support of up to ₹40 lakh. In the first round of cohort, 22 Accelerators spread across 12 states are supporting 175 startups, selected through a multilevel screening process. Success Stories of SAMRIDH supported Startups Major Objective: The SAMRIDH scheme aims to support existing and upcoming Accelerators to select and accelerate potential IT-based startups to scale. title: MeityStartupHub
text: meity_1c5c50d5e86fc876d0b3 SCHEME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.96 MeityStartupHub Scheme for Accelerating Startups around Post COVID Technology Opportunities (SASACT) https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/sasact https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/sasact seed STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 0 0 44 0 0 True OFFICIAL_SCHEME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:51:37+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN SCHEMES > SASACT Scheme for Accelerating Startups around Post COVID Technology Opportunities (SASACT) MeitY had launched SASACT (Scheme for Accelerating Start-ups Around post-COVID Technology) programme to support electronics hardware/ ICT based tech entrepreneurial initiatives of startups for developing or re-purposing technologies, tools, systems, solutions to respond to the post COVID-19 scenario emerging in the near horizon. Under the programme, eligible startups are being supported to augment and deploy into the market select technology products/ solutions within a span of 1 year. Key action areas being supported under the program are as smart/digital manufacturing including 3D printing, safety equipment, Medtech, Edtech, WFH solutions, fintech and the likes. The scheme had total outlay of Rs 10.0 Crore over a period of one year. 41 startups were selected and supported under the scheme. SASACT was executed by four Implementing Agencies (IAs), pan India for maximum coverage viz. Start up Incubation and Innovation Centre SIIC, IIT Kanpur; Society for Innovation & Entrepreneurship (SINE), IIT Bombay; Coimbatore Innovation and Business Incubator (Forge Accelerator) Coimbatore KIIT Technology Business Incubator, KIIT University, Bhubaneswar Download SASACT Report (Attachment) Get to Know Terms & Conditions Sitemap Careers Feedback About Us Contact Us Grievance Blogs Forum Privacy Policy Faqs Stay Connected Number of Users Visited MSH 2.0 Portal: 606309 Useful Links MyGov MeitY MeitY Dashboard Digital India Corporation Open Government Data National e-Governance Division India Semiconductor Mission Digital India Bhashini Startup India NITI Aayog Subscribe MeitY Startup Hub is legally registered as Independent Business Division(IBD) of Digital India Corporation. ©2023. title: MeityStartupHub
text: 6af79cf6c8a213dddce8 6af79cf6c8a213dddce8 SCHEME_MASTER_CANDIDATE 0.96 MeityStartupHub https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/tide https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/tide seed STATUS_REQUIRES_VALIDATION 200 text/html 0 0 46 0 0 True OFFICIAL_SCHEME_PATH False 0.0 2026-07-13T06:51:31+00:00 Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN SCHEMES > TIDE 2.0 Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs (TIDE 2.0) TIDE 2.0 will promote tech entrepreneurship through financial and technical support to incubators engaged in supporting ICT startups primarily engaged in using emerging technologies such as IoT, AI, Block-chain, Robotics etc. in pre-identified areas of societal relevance. The Scheme will be implemented through empowering 51 incubators in India and handholding close to 2000 tech start-ups over a period of 5 years. The scheme envisions creating a holistic ecosystem to support technology startups and incubation centers through identifying and creating necessary linkages. MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) would be setup under TIDE 2.0 to ensure synergistic linkages among the TIDE 2.0 centres, theme based incubation centres, Centre of Excellences on emerging technologies and other collaborative platforms. The MSH will also ensure crisscrossing of technology resources across the innovation ecosystem to come out with technology solutions to various societal challenges. Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs (TIDE 2.0) Scheme has been envisaged to promote tech entrepreneurship through financial and technical support to incubators engaged in supporting ICT startups using emerging technologies such as IoT, AI, Block-chain, Robotics etc. in seven pre-identified areas of societal relevance. The Scheme will be implemented through 51 incubators and handholding of approximately 2000 tech start-ups over a period of five years. It will support tech startups addressing societal challenges in seven selected thematic areas identified based on national priorities in the realms of: Success Stories of TIDE 2.0 Supported Startups Salient Features of the Scheme: TIDE 2.0 Centers: Proposals from 51 TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centres are to be obtained from institutions of higher learning and R&D organizations having incubation facilities and established linkages with large companies, industry bodies, industry clusters so as to provide necessary mentorship and possible infusion of innovative businesses into their larger business operations. To leverage the diversity in the ecosystem, it is proposed to roll out TIDE 2.0 in a layered approach. For this it is envisaged that each of the 51 TIDE 2.0 Centres will be categorized into three distinct groups as summarized below: Group 1 Centers Group 2 Centers Group 3 Centers List of TIDE 2.0 Supported Startups MSH Incubation Center Statistics: TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centre List of TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centers Important Links: Administrative Approval List of Incubation Centres under TIDE 2.0 Get to Know Terms & Conditions Sitemap Careers Feedback About Us Contact Us Grievance Blogs Forum Privacy Policy Faqs Stay Connected Number of Users Visited MSH 2.0 Portal: 606308 Useful Links MyGov MeitY MeitY Dashboard Digital India Corporation Open Government Data National e-Governance Division India Semiconductor Mission Digital India Bhashini Startup India NITI Aayog Subscribe MeitY Startup Hub is legally registered as Independent Business Division(IBD) of Digital India Corporation. ©2023. REQUIRES_ADMIN_VERIFICATION DIRECT_OR_REVIEW_REQUIRED UNKNOWN 0.600 True False Micron Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 ABOUT US SCHEMES PROGRAM NETWORKS RESOURCE MEDIA REGISTER LOGIN PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > MICRON MEITY STARTUP HUB (MSH), MICRON AND PIEDS, CREST (BITS PILANI), LAUNCH A SEMICONDUCTOR ACCELERATOR FOR EARLY-STAGE STARTUPS MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) an independent division of the Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India in partnership with Micron and PIEDS, the Technology Business Incubator and Center for Research Excellence in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST) of BITS Pilani announced today the launch of CREST accelerator program for startups in Semiconductor domain. The accelerator program will curate a cohort of startups and provide each with funding of up to Rs. 10 Lacs, with an opportunity to raise further investment at the end of the program. Mentors and advisors from Micron, MSH, and BITS Pilani shall guide the startups. The accelerator is being launched on the heels of the government’s big push to make India a semiconductor hub and an ambitious India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) by MeitY. The accelerator program will curate a cohort of startups and provide each with funding of up to Rs. 10 Lacs, with an opportunity to raise further investment at the end of the program. Mentors and advisors from Micron, MSH, and BITS Pilani shall guide the startups. The accelerator is being launched on the heels of the government’s big push to make India a semiconductor hub and an ambitious India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) by MeitY. The program aims to support 300 tech Start-Ups in 3-year duration with cohort size of 5-10 Start-Ups. SAMRIDH Program is conceptualized and being implemented to provide support to selected accelerators for extending acceleration services to start-ups along with one-to-one matching funding support of up to ₹40 lakh. In the first round of cohort, 22 Accelerators spread across 12 states are supporting 175 startups, selected through a multilevel screening process. About the program XR Startup Program is a collaboration between Meta and MeitY Startup Hub (MSH). The program aims to accelerate India’s contribution towards building the foundations of the metaverse and nurturing the development of Extended Reality (XR) technologies in India. The metaverse is the next generation of the internet. It will be a set of digital spaces, including 3D experiences that are all interconnected so people can easily move between them and will let people do things they could not do in the physical world. While the defining quality of the metaverse will be a feeling of presence, the defining technologies of the metaverse will be Extended Reality (XR), including Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR) and Mixed Reality (MR). title: Investment Playbook For Startup Incubators Final
text: © December 2025 MeitY Startup Hub, IIT Bombay, ISBA Knowledge and Resource Centre INCUBATION FUND MANAGEMENT Investment Playbook for Startup Incubators AUTHORS Dr. Panneerselvam Madangopal Poyni Bhatt Prof. Rohan Chinchwadkar December 2025 2 About The Authors: Dr. Panneersel vam Madangopal is CEO of MeitY Startup Hub and Director – Digital India Program of Ministry of Electronic and Information Technology, Government of India. His core expertise is in innovation management, business strategy and ecosystem building. He is also an educator in the domain of innovation and entrepreneurship. Poyni Bhatt is a founding team member and former CEO of SINE, the technology business incubator at IIT Bombay. She has mentored more than 250 startups within and outside SINE. She has also contributed to various central and state government departments in shaping their entrepreneurship programs. She is an Advisor- Knowledge and Resource Centre of Indian STEP & Business Incubator Association. A qualified legal and compliance professional, she has an experience of nearly three decades in industry and academia. Prof. Rohan Chinchwadkar is an Assistant Professor of Finance at the Shailesh J. Mehta School of Management, an Associate Faculty at the Desai Sethi School of Entrepreneurship and a Me mber of the Institute Innovation Council at IIT Bombay. He has worked closely with several units of the central government like the Ministry of Education and NITI Aayog (Atal Innovation Mission). He is a Research Advisor for ISBA’s Knowledge and Resource Centre. He holds a PhD from IIM Calcutta and has worked as a management consultant with McKinsey & Company. He has co -edited the book "Shifting Orbits: Decoding the Trajectory of the Indian Startup Ecosystem". *Authors’ views are personal. 3 Table of Contents Sr No Section Pg No 1 Background 4 Part - A Formation of Incubation Fund 2 Sources of Funds 7 3 Mandate and Investment Thesis 7 4 Incubation Fund Team 10 Part - B Pre-Investment Fund Management 5 Deal Sourcing 12 6 Screening and Evaluation 14 7 Investment Committee 18 8 Investment Structuring and Negotiation 19 9 Due Diligence 23 10 Investment Agreement 25 Part – C Post-Investment Fund Management 11 Fund Disbursement 28 12 Value Addition and Progress Monitoring 29 13 Exits and Self-Sustenance 30 Part - D Miscellaneous 14 Incubation Fund vs Incubation 35 15 Incubation Fund vs Angel and VC Funds 35 16 Co-investment with Other Incubators and Government Grants 36 17 Partnering with External Experts 36 4 1.Background Over the past two decades, India’s startup landscape has transformed dramatically, from a dormant ecosystem to the third-largest startup hub in the world. Central and state governments have played a catalytic role in this transformation, particularly sinc e the early 2000s. By establishing startup hubs and business incubators within academic institutions and knowledge centers, they laid the foundation for a vibrant entrepreneurial ecosystem. Government support has grown consistently since the start of this century, with a particularly strong push over the past decade. Among the most significant initiatives has been the launch of schemes and programs to fund proof-of-concept development and provide seed capital to early- stage startups and innovators. The Technology Development Board (TDB) under the Department of Science and Technology (DST) and the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) were among the first government bodies to introduce startup investment schemes, which were implemented through incubators. These efforts came at a time when India had very limited private investor participation in the startup space. As India’s startup investment environment matured, various government departments scaled up their funding efforts to make patien t capital available to early -stage startups. However, government bodies typically do not invest directly in startups. They do so through incubators. Technology business incubators play a pivotal role in nurturing nascent ideas into commercially viable busi nesses. The traditional model of incubation, primarily providing mentorship and workspace, has evolved into a more hands -on, investment -oriented approach. Today, many incubators actively participate in government startup funding schemes, using these grants to invest in startups through instruments such as equity, loans or convertible notes. In doing so, incubators become long-term stakeholders in the companies they support. In this emerging model, funding has become a core value proposition of incubators. Consequently, incubators are now expected to develop fund management capabilities similar to commercial investors. While these grants are typically non -repayable and failure to recover investments carries no financial penalty, incubator managers must still adopt an investor’s mindset when deploying public or corporate social responsibility (CSR) funds. title: MeitY Startup Hub Partners with State Bank of India
text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A REGISTER LOGIN ABOUT US Organisation Profile Team DIC MSH logo usage guidelines Apply for MSH logo Newsletter Contact Us Innovation Patent & IPR Support SCHEMES SAMRIDH TIDE 2.0 SITAA SASACT GENESIS PROGRAM MSH Corporate SBI TALLY RAZORPAY PARTNERSHIP MATHWORKS MICRON SAMSUNG "SOLVE FOR TOMORROW" GOOGLE APPSCALE 2023 IMPACT BHUMI - BSF DRISHTI - SSB IHMCL META XR Startup Program Global Startup Bridge IFTECH BRUSSELS 2025 VIVATECH 2023 VIVATECH 2022 COE INCUBATOR NETWORKS Startups Incubators Mentors Accelerators COE Investors RESOURCE Labs Venue Space Co working space Equipment Startup School IPR Support MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery Events G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More Publications CAREERS MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery EVENTS G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More REGISTER LOGIN PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > SBI MeitY Startup Hub Partners with State Bank of India Enabling Startup Growth Through Strategic Financial Access As part of its mission to build a robust and inclusive startup ecosystem, MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) has partnered with the State Bank of India (SBI) — India’s largest public sector bank — to empower startups with the right mix of financial access, strategic mentorship, and ecosystem enablement. Building Awareness: Beyond Banking SBI joins hands with MSH not just as a banking institution, but as a committed growth partner. Through this collaboration, startups will benefit from: Access to financial literacy programs and mentorship Exposure to market insights and capital-readiness support Guidance on financial planning, compliance, and risk management This awareness-focused approach ensures that founders make informed, confident financial decisions as they scale. Enhancing Accessibility: Financing What’s Next With this partnership, startups can tap into SBI’s startup-centric offerings, including: Equity-linked funding opportunities Tailored credit lines and working capital support Support through SBI’s pan-India branch and digital network A Step Toward Inclusive Startup Growth By bridging the gap between innovation and financial infrastructure, the MSH x SBI partnership is set to power a new wave of entrepreneurship, from metropolitan hubs to Tier II/III cities.Together, we aim to create an enabling environment where startups are not only funded, but also mentored, connected, and equipped for sustainable growth. Are you a startup looking to scale with the right financial partner? Explore tailored offerings and startup-focused solutions from SBI here: SBI Startup Portal . This is your gateway to growth Get in Touch Looking to explore SBI’s startup offerings or need personalized assistance? Connect with dedicated SBI Startup Branches, listed below to get the right guidance and support tailored to your startup’s needs. Specialised Startup Branch, Hyderabad Email: Sbi.64338@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Chennai Email: Sbi.64320@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Bengaluru Email: Sbi.64074@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Pune Email: Sbi.64614@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Mumbai Email: Sbi.64369@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Gurugram Email: Sbi.61808@sbi.co.in Get to Know Terms & Conditions Sitemap Careers Feedback About Us Contact Us Grievance Blogs Forum Privacy Policy Faqs Stay Connected Number of Users Visited MSH 2.0 Portal: 607346 Useful Links MyGov MeitY MeitY Dashboard Digital India Corporation Open Government Data National e-Governance Division India Semiconductor Mission Digital India Bhashini Startup India NITI Aayog Subscribe MeitY Startup Hub is legally registered as Independent Business Division(IBD) of Digital India Corporation. ©2023. title: Google Appscale Academy 2023 Impact
text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A REGISTER LOGIN ABOUT US Organisation Profile Team DIC MSH logo usage guidelines Apply for MSH logo Newsletter Contact Us Innovation Patent & IPR Support SCHEMES SAMRIDH TIDE 2.0 SITAA SASACT GENESIS PROGRAM MSH Corporate SBI TALLY RAZORPAY PARTNERSHIP MATHWORKS MICRON SAMSUNG "SOLVE FOR TOMORROW" GOOGLE APPSCALE 2023 IMPACT BHUMI - BSF DRISHTI - SSB IHMCL META XR Startup Program Global Startup Bridge IFTECH BRUSSELS 2025 VIVATECH 2023 VIVATECH 2022 COE INCUBATOR NETWORKS Startups Incubators Mentors Accelerators COE Investors RESOURCE Labs Venue Space Co working space Equipment Startup School IPR Support MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery Events G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More Publications CAREERS MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery EVENTS G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More REGISTER LOGIN PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > GOOGLE APPSCALE ACADEMY 2023 IMPACT Google Appscale Academy 2023 Impact Google Appscale Academy the Class of 2023, was executed with 100 startups selected from over 950 applications, represents a diverse range of sectors, including education, health, social impact, gaming, agriculture, and more. These innovative startups have harnessed technology to address critical needs, making a tangible impact on society. Appscale Academy has been instrumental in training and mentoring early to mid-stage startups, empowering them to build high-quality apps for both local and global markets. A comprehensive Google Appscale Academy 2023 VC Handbook showcasing the success stories of the program. The VC handbook serves as a consolidated repository, featuring details of all the selected startups. Indian developers continue to push boundaries by building innovative solutions for many complex challenges. Be it apps supporting diverse communities or global games emerging from beyond metro cities, we’re seeing inspiring local developers across the country underscore India’s rise as a powerful app development hub. Indian developers continue to push boundaries by building innovative solutions for many complex challenges. Be it apps supporting diverse communities or global games emerging from beyond metro cities, we’re seeing inspiring local developers across the country underscore India’s rise as a powerful app development hub. The program is outlined with a customized curriculum, including virtual instructor-led webinars, self-learning material, and mentorship sessions with leading local and global professionals from the industry. he curated, 6-month training program will identify, support and nurture promising Indian startups to supercharge their growth on Android. Google Appscale Academy 2023 Roadshows To build general awareness for the program amongst the broader ecosystem, especially in beyondmetro cities and supercharge their growth on Android, MeitY Startup Hub and Google organized 5 roadshows in 5 cities (Surat, Indore, Coimbatore, Jaipur, Gangtok) with the 5 incubation centres as implementation partners (AIC-RAISE - Coimbatore, Incub8 - Surat, Headstart, SGSITS-Shri Govindram Institute of Technology and Science - Indore, JECRC - Jaipur Engineering College and Research Centre, Atal Incubation Centre Sikkim Manipal University Technology Business Incubation Foundation (AIC-SMUTBI) - Gangtok). Google Appscale Academy 2023 Top 100 Reveal Event at India Habitat Centre Hon'ble Joint Secretary, MeitY, Shri Akash Tripathi unveiled the class of 2023 of Appscale Academy 2023. He added "India, currently holding the prestigious Presidency of G20 nations, has grown from being the tenth largest economy ten years ago to the fifth largest today. India has been the fastest growing major economy for the third year in a row—assured for further growth, driven strongly by, among other factors, a vigorous and diverse technology ecosystem. Google and MeitY's partnership through MSH have added a lot of value to the Indian startup ecosystem. My Best wishes to the Class of 2023" The chosen early to mid-stage startups will be guided by Appscale Academy to build high-quality apps for the world. The 100 startups were chosen from over 950 applications following an in-depth selection process that considered their creative ideas and innovation, product quality, product scalability, and talent diversity. 50% of the cohort representing major sectors like education, health, social, agriculture and more come from beyond metro cities including Varanasi, Ernakulam,Surat, Dehradun, Allahabad and more. title: MeitY Startup Hub and MathWorks Partner to Accelerate Deep Tech Innovation
text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A REGISTER LOGIN ABOUT US Organisation Profile Team DIC MSH logo usage guidelines Apply for MSH logo Newsletter Contact Us Innovation Patent & IPR Support SCHEMES SAMRIDH TIDE 2.0 SITAA SASACT GENESIS PROGRAM MSH Corporate SBI TALLY RAZORPAY PARTNERSHIP MATHWORKS MICRON SAMSUNG "SOLVE FOR TOMORROW" GOOGLE APPSCALE 2023 IMPACT BHUMI - BSF DRISHTI - SSB IHMCL META XR Startup Program Global Startup Bridge IFTECH BRUSSELS 2025 VIVATECH 2023 VIVATECH 2022 COE INCUBATOR NETWORKS Startups Incubators Mentors Accelerators COE Investors RESOURCE Labs Venue Space Co working space Equipment Startup School IPR Support MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery Events G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More Publications CAREERS MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery EVENTS G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More REGISTER LOGIN PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > MATHWORKS MeitY Startup Hub and MathWorks Partner to Accelerate Deep Tech Innovation MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) has signed a Statement of Intent (SoI) with MathWorks to bolster India’s deep tech startup ecosystem, providing access to state-of-the-art computational tools, advanced simulation models, and world-class training resources. As part of this partnership, MathWorks will provide complimentary access to over 100 products under its Accelerator Program , including MATLAB® and Simulink , for 12 months to all MeitY associated startups. This invaluable resource will empower startups to develop and refine their innovations with powerful computational capabilities. Beyond software access, participating startups will benefit from Technical Mentorship: Expert guidance to navigate complex technical challenges. Specialized Training: Tailored programs to maximize the utilization of MathWorks tools. Discounted Professional Access: Continued access to MathWorks products at preferential rates post-program. To further enhance industry engagement, MSH and MathWorks will conduct collaborative physical workshops at leading incubation centres across India. These workshops will empower startups, researchers, and industry professionals with the practical skills needed to address real-world challenges and accelerate innovation. Furthermore, MathWorks will offer advanced training at discounted fees, supporting faster innovation cycles and market access. Startups will also benefit from participation in MathWorks-organized events, such as MATLAB EXPO and webinars, along with opportunities to showcase their solutions on global platforms. We are thrilled to partner with MeitY Startup Hub to empower deep tech startups driving innovation in electrification, automation, and AI,” said Mr. Sunil Motwani, Country Manager, MathWorks India. “Through our tools and expertise, we aim to accelerate the development of transformative technologies that will shape the future of mobility and beyond. At MSH, we are excited to announce our commitment to empowering startups with advanced MathWorks products,” said Mr. Pannerselvam, CEO, MSH. “This partnership will support innovations that will provide startups with a significant edge in key technologies such as Artificial Intelligence, Wireless Communication, and Automation. This strategic deep tech partnership bridges the gap between technology and entrepreneurship, providing Indian startups with the essential resources to innovate, scale, and tackle global challenges. Together, MSH and MathWorks are building a thriving, future-ready startup ecosystem in India. title: MeitY Startup Hub (MSH), Micron and PIEDS, CREST (BITS Pilani), launch a Semiconductor Accelerator for Early-Stage Startups
text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A REGISTER LOGIN ABOUT US Organisation Profile Team DIC MSH logo usage guidelines Apply for MSH logo Newsletter Contact Us Innovation Patent & IPR Support SCHEMES SAMRIDH TIDE 2.0 SITAA SASACT GENESIS PROGRAM MSH Corporate SBI TALLY RAZORPAY PARTNERSHIP MATHWORKS MICRON SAMSUNG "SOLVE FOR TOMORROW" GOOGLE APPSCALE 2023 IMPACT BHUMI - BSF DRISHTI - SSB IHMCL META XR Startup Program Global Startup Bridge IFTECH BRUSSELS 2025 VIVATECH 2023 VIVATECH 2022 COE INCUBATOR NETWORKS Startups Incubators Mentors Accelerators COE Investors RESOURCE Labs Venue Space Co working space Equipment Startup School IPR Support MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery Events G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More Publications CAREERS MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery EVENTS G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More REGISTER LOGIN PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > MICRON MeitY Startup Hub (MSH), Micron and PIEDS, CREST (BITS Pilani), launch a Semiconductor Accelerator for Early-Stage Startups MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) an independent division of the Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India in partnership with Micron and PIEDS, the Technology Business Incubator and Center for Research Excellence in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST) of BITS Pilani announced today the launch of CREST accelerator program for startups in Semiconductor domain. The accelerator program will curate a cohort of startups and provide each with funding of up to Rs. 10 Lacs, with an opportunity to raise further investment at the end of the program. Mentors and advisors from Micron, MSH, and BITS Pilani shall guide the startups. The accelerator is being launched on the heels of the government’s big push to make India a semiconductor hub and an ambitious India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) by MeitY. The startups shall be able to use tools, labs, and facilities at Micron facilities and at the Centre for Research and Entrepreneurship in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST), BITS Pilani, Hyderabad campus, and the network of MeitY supported labs and facilities across India. Shri Rajnish Kumar, CEO, MeitY Startup Hub, said, “We congratulate the PIDES team for their initiative to support semiconductor startups with funding, mentorship, and access to Micron’s facilities for product development. Together with Micron, we are committed to nurturing the next generation of pioneers in semiconductor technology” Sachin Arya, Head, PIEDS said, “The startups will play an integral role in India’s ambitious Semiconductor Mission, and further strengthen India’s indigenous design and development capabilities. We are excited to support promising startups in Semiconductors and utilize BITS Pilani’s capabilities in Semiconductor research and innovation for overall ecosystem development.” Prof. Sanket Goel, Dean (Research & Innovation) and Convenor, CREST, said, “The Semiconductor Accelerator, will bridge the critical gap between innovative ideas and market reality for early-stage startups in chip design. By leveraging resources, mentorship, and industry connections, this dynamic program will empower students and recent graduate entrepreneurs to thrive. By providing access to seed funding, state-of-the-art labs, and a collaborative learning environment, the Accelerator will foster the next generation of innovators, shaping the future of this transformative technology.” Interested startups can apply to the CREST accelerator program by logging on to the MSH’s website https://msh.meity.gov.in/ PIEDS PIED Society is a leading platform that helps entrepreneurs get access to resources that can help them come together and innovate the future of India. It is a Technology Business Incubator at BITS Pilani which was set up in 2004 with support from Department of Science and Technology, Government of India, and have incubated many campus and alumni driven startups. With a mission to support every innovative idea that can scale to be successful, PIED Society works with startups from all BITS campuses, its alumni, and beyond. Micron Micron is a world leader in innovative memory solutions that transform how the world uses information. For over 45 years, Micron has been instrumental to the world’s most significant technology advancements, delivering optimal memory and storage systems for a broad range of applications. CREST With a vision to make BITS Pilani as the leading academic institution for Semiconductor Education, Research and Innovation in India, Center for Research Excellence in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST) was launched in 2023. Since then, CREST is on the forefront of diversified activities, including professional development

The second season of Samsung Solve for Tomorrow was inaugurated by Shri. Alkesh Kumar Sharma, Secretary, Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY) in the presence of Prof. Rangan Banerjee, Director, IIT Delhi, Mr. Jeet Vijay, CEO, MeitY Startup Hub and Mr. JongBum Park, President & CEO, Samsung Southwest Asia. Message by Honorable Secretary MeitY, Shri. Alkesh Kumar Sharma “The vision of the Government of India is to promote a vibrant ecosystem of innovation and entrepreneurship in the country, and youth are at the centre of this. title: XR Startup Program
text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A REGISTER LOGIN ABOUT US Organisation Profile Team DIC MSH logo usage guidelines Apply for MSH logo Newsletter Contact Us Innovation Patent & IPR Support SCHEMES SAMRIDH TIDE 2.0 SITAA SASACT GENESIS PROGRAM MSH Corporate SBI TALLY RAZORPAY PARTNERSHIP MATHWORKS MICRON SAMSUNG "SOLVE FOR TOMORROW" GOOGLE APPSCALE 2023 IMPACT BHUMI - BSF DRISHTI - SSB IHMCL META XR Startup Program Global Startup Bridge IFTECH BRUSSELS 2025 VIVATECH 2023 VIVATECH 2022 COE INCUBATOR NETWORKS Startups Incubators Mentors Accelerators COE Investors RESOURCE Labs Venue Space Co working space Equipment Startup School IPR Support MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery Events G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More Publications CAREERS MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery EVENTS G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More REGISTER LOGIN PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 Shortlisted Startups XR Startup Program A program to discover, nurture, and accelerate XR technology startups across India. About the program XR Startup Program is a collaboration between Meta and MeitY Startup Hub (MSH). The program aims to accelerate India’s contribution towards building the foundations of the metaverse and nurturing the development of Extended Reality (XR) technologies in India. The metaverse is the next generation of the internet. It will be a set of digital spaces, including 3D experiences that are all interconnected so people can easily move between them and will let people do things they could not do in the physical world. While the defining quality of the metaverse will be a feeling of presence, the defining technologies of the metaverse will be Extended Reality (XR), including Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR) and Mixed Reality (MR). Click here to read more Accelerator About the Accelerator The Accelerator will nurture and foster 40 early-stage startups working with XR technologies through a well-designed 6-month program. Each startup will get access to a number of benefits, including a grant of ₹ 20,00,000, mentorship by industry experts and researchers, access to a network of potential strategic partners and investors, technology and infrastructure support, and opportunities to participate and present at relevant industry events. North zone - FITT South zone - FITT East zone - FITT West zone - FITT Grand Challenge About the Grand Challenge The Grand Challenge is aimed at supporting early-stage innovators to upscale from the R&D phase to developing workable prototypes and Minimum Viable Products (MVPs). It will include 4 sector-specific challenges to advance the XR technology ecosystem in India. A total of 80 shortlisted innovators will receive a grant of ₹ 50,000 and an opportunity to attend the bootcamp. Of the 80 shortlisted innovators, 16 innovators will be supported with grants worth ₹ 20,00,000 to help them develop MVP(s)/ prototypes. Benefits include mentorship by industry experts and researchers, access to a network of potential strategic partners and investors, technology and infrastructure support, and opportunities to participate and present at industry events. North zone - FITT South zone - FITT East zone - FITT West zone - FITT Implementation partners Frequently Asked Questions How does one apply to the program? Accelerator – You can select any 1 of the implementation partners mentioned above and click on the relevant link to learn more and apply. Participants will be required to visit the facility/ campus of the partner; hence, it is best to choose the partner situated in or closer to your region. Grand Challenge – Depending on your focus area of innovation, you can select any 1 of the implementation partners mentioned above and click on the relevant link to learn more and apply. What is the selection process of the program? Each implementation partner will establish a 3-member panel to evaluate and select the applications. Applications will then be discussed with the steering committee established by Meta in collaboration with MSH. More details are available on the website of the relevant implementation partner. What are the program benefits? Under the Accelerator, 40 selected startups will receive the following benefits: Grant of ₹ 20,00,000 Mentorship by industry experts and researchers Access to a network of potential strategic partners and investors, along with technology and infrastructure support Opportunities to participate and present at industry events Whom should one contact in case of queries? The respective implementation partner can be contacted for queries on email: FITT: tidefitt@gmail.com CIE IIIT-H: incubation@iiith.org and cieoutreach@iiit.ac.in GUSEC: programs@gusec.edu.in SMU-TBI: contact@smutbi.c

This edition aimed to connect Indian startups with the European innovation ecosystem through cross-border collaboration, policy engagement, and knowledge exchange.The program focused on enabling market access, forging corporate and government partnerships, unlocking investment opportunities, facilitating technology exchange, and deepening ecosystem engagement. The Brussels edition reaffirmed MeitY Startup Hub’s vision of enabling Indian startups to scale globally through strategic partnerships, international exposure, and ecosystem-level collaboration. With the MeitY Global Startup Bridge initiative, MeitY Startup Hub positions itself as a strategic facilitator, bridging Indian deep-tech startups with the global market. The initiative addresses core startup needs such as international market access, investor and corporate connections, regulatory guidance, R&D collaboration, and ecosystem visibility. But beyond access and funding, the MeitY Startup Hub also opens doors to the right people, platforms, and partnerships, ensuring startups are not just supported, but strategically connected to scale globally. If you're a startup looking to grow beyond borders, not just through research or capital, but by plugging into the right global networks, this is your gateway. The signing of MoU between MeitY Startup Hub and hub.brussels at the Indian Embassy, by Dr. Paneerselvam Madanagopal , CEO, MeitY Startup Hub, and Mr. Bart Vandeputte , Trade and Invest Commissioner, hub.brussels, in the presence of Ambassador Shri Saurabh Kumar , underscoring a shared commitment to foster India–Belgium innovation partnerships. A key highlight was the signing of an MoU between Indian startup IG Drones and Belgian firm VoxelSensors , aimed at advancing bilateral collaboration in drone and sensor technologies. - Click on the tabs to know more: Why Brussels? The program was initiated by the signature of the Indo-France Digital Roadmap by Honourable Prime Minister of India, Shri. Narendra Modi, and Honourable President of France, Emmanuel Macron in August 2019. IFTECH was sponsored by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), and La French Tech. It was managed and implemented by MeitY Startup Hub and LINK (formerly NUMA Bengaluru). It was supported by Software Technology Parks of India (STPI), The French Embassy in India and Business France. title: VIVATECH 2022
text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A REGISTER LOGIN ABOUT US Organisation Profile Team DIC MSH logo usage guidelines Apply for MSH logo Newsletter Contact Us Innovation Patent & IPR Support SCHEMES SAMRIDH TIDE 2.0 SITAA SASACT GENESIS PROGRAM MSH Corporate SBI TALLY RAZORPAY PARTNERSHIP MATHWORKS MICRON SAMSUNG "SOLVE FOR TOMORROW" GOOGLE APPSCALE 2023 IMPACT BHUMI - BSF DRISHTI - SSB IHMCL META XR Startup Program Global Startup Bridge IFTECH BRUSSELS 2025 VIVATECH 2023 VIVATECH 2022 COE INCUBATOR NETWORKS Startups Incubators Mentors Accelerators COE Investors RESOURCE Labs Venue Space Co working space Equipment Startup School IPR Support MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery Events G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More Publications CAREERS MEDIA Press Releases Social Media News Gallery EVENTS G20 DIA Overview Exhibition Program at Glance The Summit Speakers & Juries Gallery More REGISTER LOGIN PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > Global Startup Bridge > VIVATECH VIVATECH 2022 VIVATECH is Europe’s biggest startup and tech event which accelerates innovation by connecting startups, tech leaders, major corporations, and investors responding to the world’s biggest challenges. From keynote speakers and exhibits to labs and live experiences, VIVATECH 2022 was a celebration of today’s innovative technologies that have the power to transform the future. India was the “Country of the Year” partner of VIVATECH 2022 in Paris. MeitY Startup Hub delegation participated in the 4-day event and the startups were showcased at the India’s pavilion. The pavilion highlighted the Indian startup ecosystem, partnership ecosystem, and provided the platform for startups to engage with investors, corporates, and other prospective partners.The conference was inaugurated by Shri. Ashwini Vaishnaw, Minister of Railways, Communication, Electronics and Information Technology.
